competency_groups.title: Fundamental Concepts and Measurement
competency_groups.description: Ability to understand the nature of matter, apply laws of chemical combination, perform stoichiometric calculations using mole concept, and handle measurement uncertainties.
competencies: Explain the importance of Chemistry in various disciplines and career paths.
Distinguish between macroscopic, molecular, and symbolic representations of chemical phenomena.
Apply Dalton's Atomic Theory to explain atomic and molecular masses.
Perform stoichiometric calculations involving percentage composition and molar mass.
Identify patterns in laws of chemical combination.

competency_groups.title: Atomic Structure and Quantum Mechanics
competency_groups.description: Ability to trace the development from Bohr's model to quantum mechanical models, understand sub-atomic particles, and apply quantum numbers.
competencies: Describe the discovery of sub-atomic particles (electron, proton, neutron).
Analyze the limitations and successes of Bohr's Model for Hydrogen Atom.
Explain the transition towards Quantum Mechanical Models of the atom.
Apply quantum mechanical concepts to describe electron distribution.

competency_groups.title: Periodic Classification and Trends
competency_groups.description: Ability to classify elements based on electronic configuration, understand the genesis of periodic classification, and predict trends in properties.
competencies: Justify the need for classifying elements and explain the Modern Periodic Law.
Determine nomenclature for elements with atomic number > 100.
Correlate electronic configuration (s-, p-, d-, f- blocks) with element classification.
Predict periodic trends in physical and chemical properties across periods and down groups.
competency_groups.unit_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
competency_groups.chapter_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

competency_groups.title: Chemical Bonding Theories
competency_groups.description: Ability to apply various bonding theories (Kossel-Lewis, VSEPR, Valence Bond, Molecular Orbital) to predict molecular structure and properties.
competencies: Differentiate between Ionic, Covalent, and Coordinate bonds using Kossel-Lewis approach.
Predict molecular geometry using VSEPR Theory based on electron pair repulsion.
Explain hybridization concepts (sp, sp2, sp3) in the context of Valence Bond Theory.
Apply Molecular Orbital Theory to explain bonding in homonuclear diatomic molecules.

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamic Principles and Spontaneity
competency_groups.description: Ability to analyze energy changes in chemical reactions, calculate enthalpy variations, and determine reaction spontaneity using Gibbs Energy.
competencies: Define thermodynamic terms (system, surroundings, state functions).
Calculate internal energy change (∆U) and enthalpy change (∆H) via calorimetry.
Distinguish between exothermic and endothermic reactions based on reaction enthalpies.
Predict the spontaneity of a process using Gibbs Energy Change.

competency_groups.title: Chemical and Ionic Equilibrium
competency_groups.description: Ability to analyze dynamic equilibrium in physical and chemical processes, apply Le Chatelier's principle, and solve ionic equilibrium problems.
competencies: Distinguish between homogeneous and heterogeneous equilibria.
Apply the Law of Chemical Equilibrium and calculate equilibrium constants (K).
Relate Gibbs Energy to Reaction Quotient (Q) and Equilibrium Constant (K).
Predict shifts in equilibrium due to changes in concentration, temperature, or pressure.

competency_groups.title: Redox Reactions and Electrode Processes
competency_groups.description: Ability to identify oxidation-reduction processes, assign oxidation numbers, and understand redox reactions in electrochemical contexts.
competencies: Define oxidation and reduction from classical and electron transfer perspectives.
Assign oxidation numbers to elements in compounds and ions.
Balance redox equations using the ion-electron method or oxidation number change.

competency_groups.title: Organic Chemistry Fundamentals and Mechanisms
competency_groups.description: Ability to represent organic structures, apply IUPAC nomenclature, understand isomerism, and analyze reaction mechanisms.
competencies: Represent tetravalence of carbon using various structural representations (Lewis, wedge-dash).
Apply IUPAC rules for naming organic compounds including functional groups.
Identify different types of isomers (structural, stereoisomerism).
Analyze fundamental concepts in reaction mechanisms and purification methods.
competency_groups.unit_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques
competency_groups.chapter_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Hydrocarbons Classification and Reactivity
competency_groups.description: Ability to classify hydrocarbons, understand their preparation methods, physical properties, and chemical reactivity including aromaticity.
competencies: Classify alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, and aromatic compounds based on structure.

competency_groups.title: Solutions and Colligative Properties
competency_groups.description: Ability to express solution concentrations, understand solubility principles, and apply colligative properties for molar mass determination.
competencies: Express concentration of solutions using various units (molarity, molality, etc.).

competency_groups.title: Electrochemistry and Energy Conversion
competency_groups.description: Ability to analyze galvanic and electrolytic cells, apply Nernst equation, and understand corrosion and battery mechanisms.
competencies: Differentiate between Galvanic and Electrolytic cells.

competency_groups.title: Chemical Kinetics and Reaction Rates
competency_groups.description: Ability to determine reaction rates, apply integrated rate equations, and explain temperature dependence using collision theory.
competencies: Define factors influencing the rate of a chemical reaction.

competency_groups.title: d and f Block Elements Properties
competency_groups.description: Ability to describe the position, electronic configuration, general properties, and applications of transition elements and inner transition metals.
competencies: Describe the characteristics of d-block (transition) and f-block (lanthanoids/actinoids) elements.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit 4: d and f Block Elements
competency_groups.chapter_name: Unit 4: d and f Block Elements

competency_groups.title: Coordination Chemistry Fundamentals
competency_groups.description: Ability to apply Werner's theory, nomenclature rules, isomerism concepts, and bonding theories (Valence Bond/Crystal Field) in coordination compounds.
competencies: Apply IUPAC nomenclature for coordination compounds.

competency_groups.title: Haloalkanes and Haloarenes Reactivity
competency_groups.description: Ability to classify halo compounds, understand C-X bond nature, and predict preparation methods and chemical reactions.
competencies: Analyze the classification and nomenclature of haloalkanes and haloarenes.

competency_groups.title: Alcohols, Phenols, and Ethers Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Ability to classify oxygen-containing organic compounds, understand their structures, nomenclature, and commercial importance.
competencies: Distinguish between alcohols, phenols, and ethers based on structure.

competency_groups.title: Carbonyl Compounds Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Ability to understand the chemistry of aldehydes, ketones, and carboxylic acids including preparation methods and reactions.
competencies: Differentiate between carbonyl groups in aldehydes/ketones versus carboxyl groups.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit 8: Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids
competency_groups.chapter_name: Unit 8: Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids

competency_groups.title: Amines Chemistry and Diazonium Salts
competency_groups.description: Ability to classify amines, understand their preparation methods, physical properties, and the significance of diazonium salts in synthesis.
competencies: Explain the structure and classification of amines.

competency_groups.title: Biomolecules Structure and Function
competency_groups.description: Ability to classify carbohydrates, proteins, vitamins, nucleic acids, and hormones based on their structure and biological roles.
competencies: Identify the structural components of major biomolecules.

competency_groups.title: Surface Chemistry and Colloids
competency_groups.description: Ability to distinguish between true solutions, colloids, and suspensions; understand adsorption phenomena and emulsions.
competencies: Differentiate lyophilic and lyophobic sols.

competency_groups.title: Isolation of Elements Principles
competency_groups.description: Ability to understand the principles of extraction, concentration, oxidation-reduction methods, and refining processes.
competencies: Explain general principles and methods for isolation of elements.

competency_groups.title: Polymers Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Ability to classify polymers, understand polymerization mechanisms (homopolymers vs copolymers), and identify examples.
competencies: Distinguish between homopolymers and copolymers.

competency_groups.title: Chemistry in Everyday Life Applications
competency_groups.description: Ability to identify chemical substances used in medicines and food, understanding their functions as analgesics, preservatives, etc.
competencies: Identify chemicals used as antiseptics, antibiotics, or antioxidants.

competency_groups.title: Laboratory Techniques and Safety
competency_groups.description: Ability to perform basic laboratory operations such as cutting glass, boring corks, crystallization, and pH determination safely.
competencies: Demonstrate proficiency in handling laboratory apparatus (beakers, burettes, pipettes).
Perform qualitative analysis for anions and cations.

competency_groups.title: Scientific Inquiry and Project Work
competency_groups.description: Ability to design investigations, collect data from laboratory testing or other sources, and analyze findings related to environmental or chemical issues.
competencies: Design a scientific investigation involving laboratory testing.
